name: Patient Records & Consent (MCNZ aligned)
description: Reference policy aligned with MCNZ guidance on patient records.

data.text: Clinical notes are clear, accurate, and detailed enough for another doctor to continue care.
Records are retained for at least 10 years from the date of last consultation.
Informed consent is documented for procedures, prescribing of high-risk medicines, and any treatment with significant risks.
Prescribing records identify the medicine, dose, frequency, duration, and indication.
Discharge instructions and follow-up plans are recorded in language the patient can understand.
Each entry identifies the doctor; corrections by addendum only.

clauses.title: Notes sufficient for handover
clauses.body: Clinical notes must be clear, accurate, and detailed enough for another doctor to continue care.

clauses.title: 10-year retention
clauses.body: Records must be retained for at least 10 years from the date of last consultation.
clauses.source_citation: MCNZ Statement on retention of patient records

clauses.title: Informed consent documented
clauses.body: Informed consent must be documented for procedures, prescribing of high-risk medicines, and any treatment with significant risks.

clauses.title: Prescribing records complete
clauses.body: Prescribing records must identify the medicine, dose, frequency, duration, and indication.

clauses.title: Continuity of care documented
clauses.body: You must record discharge instructions and follow-up plans in language the patient understands.

clauses.title: Doctor sign-off identified
clauses.body: Each entry must identify the doctor; corrections by addendum only.
